title: Protocol for Electronic von Frey
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ec7b7","text":"The von Frey method measures mechanical sensitivity and allodynia. This protocol outlines the procedure for assessing the mechanical sensitivity threshold in rats using the electronic von Frey.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"fcu61","text":"Environment during testing must be quiet to reduce animals' stress","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"br2si","text":"Clean the clear boxes and metal grid with quatricide in between each set of animals","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"bgr28","text":"Electronic von Frey apparatus (Ugo Basile® Dynamic Plantar Aesthesiometer 37450)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1he1u","text":"Elevated horizontal grid","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f4g7a","text":"Clear rat boxes/enclosures","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5e0qh","text":"Quatricide (cleaner)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7j8a4","text":"Paper towels","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1afnf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"13if5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Remove metal grid and clear boxes off the elevated platform
Place touch stimulator on testing surface and remove the needle cap
Place the metal grid back on the legs making sure the grid does not touch the needle
Place the clear boxes back on top of the grid making sure the opaque sides are placed to prevent animals from seeing each other
Switch on the Ugo Basile® Dynamic Plantar Aesthesiometer 37450 machine Power button located at the rear of machine
Press F4 ("ESC") once, then F1 twice ("OPR" then "TST" ) to display the testing screen Testing screen

Remove the lid and carefully place one rat per clear box, then place the lids on Up to 6 animals can be tested at once If the animals are weighing down the grid causing it to touch the needle, take out 1 or 2 animals and test less animals at a time and spread them evenly across the grid The touch stimulator should move across the surface freely without the needle touching the grid while testing a set of animals
Allow rats to habituate in the clear boxes for 10 minutes
Measure the left hind paw first for each animal, then go back and measure the right hind paw for all animals.

Position the needle under the center of the hind paw, then press the button on the touch stimulator to begin the force measurement Make sure the needle goes through the net and actually touches the paw. Black dots indicate testing area. Avoid testing the pads (bumps).

When paw withdrawal occurs due to pain, the needle will retract and the force and time will display on the machine. Record these measurements. The animal must be still during the application of the needle tip.

Measure each hind paw for all animals for Trial 1. Then repeat for Trial 2, and Trial 3. Once you have 3 Trials you are done. If an animal is walking or moving not because of pain, move on to the next animal and come back to it later. Wait at least 2 minutes before retesting the same paw.
After testing is complete, return animals to home cages
Clean the clear boxes and grid before testing another set of animals
Clean all parts of the electronic von Frey (boxes, grid, needle) and make sure to recap the delicate von Frey needle.
creator: Lani Tieu
